annotations.label: Generalized hypergeometric series

anchor.snippet: a generalized hypergeometric series is a power series in which the ratio of successive coefficients indexed by n is a rational function of n

annotations.note: Mathlib only has the ₂F₁ case (`ordinaryHypergeometricSeries`); the general pFq series is not formalized.

annotations.label: Generalized hypergeometric function

anchor.snippet: The series, if convergent, defines a generalized hypergeometric function

annotations.note: Only the ₂F₁ instance `ordinaryHypergeometric` is defined; general pFq is absent.

annotations.label: Hypergeometric series (formal definition)

anchor.snippet: A hypergeometric series is formally defined as a power series

annotations.note: Only the ₂F₁ formal series is in Mathlib via `ordinaryHypergeometricSeries`.

annotations.label: Exponential function series

anchor.snippet: For example, in the case of the series for the exponential function

annotations.note: `exp_eq_tsum` gives the standard `∑ xⁿ/n!` expansion.

annotations.label: Pochhammer symbol notation

anchor.snippet: Using the rising factorial or Pochhammer symbol

annotations.note: `ascPochhammer S n = X(X+1)…(X+n-1)` is the rising factorial / Pochhammer symbol.

annotations.label: Hypergeometric function (analytic)

anchor.snippet: Such a function, and its analytic continuations , is called the hypergeometric function

annotations.note: Only the ₂F₁ analytic function is defined; no analytic continuation theory or general pFq.

annotations.label: Incomplete gamma function asymptotic

anchor.snippet: for example the incomplete gamma function has the asymptotic expansion

annotations.note: No incomplete gamma function or its asymptotic expansion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bilateral hypergeometric series

anchor.snippet: is called the bilateral hypergeometric series

annotations.note: No bilateral hypergeometric series in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polynomial termination when a_j non-positive integer

anchor.snippet: If any a j is a non-positive integer (0, −1, −2, etc.) then the series only has a finite number of terms and is, in fact, a polynomial of degree

annotations.note: Proven for ₂F₁ (`ordinaryHypergeometricSeries_eq_zero_of_neg_nat`); no general pFq version.

annotations.label: Undefined when b_k non-positive integer

anchor.snippet: If any b k is a non-positive integer (excepting the previous case with b k < a j ) then the denominators become 0 and the series is undefined

annotations.note: Mathlib's ₂F₁ assigns junk values via inverse-of-zero, so the undefinedness statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Convergence for p < q+1

anchor.snippet: If p < q + 1 then the ratio of coefficients tends to zero. This implies that the series converges for any finite value of z and thus defines an entire function of z

annotations.note: General pFq is not defined in Mathlib, so the entire-function statement is absent.

annotations.label: Convergence for p = q+1

anchor.snippet: If p = q + 1 then the ratio of coefficients tends to one. This implies that the series converges for

annotations.note: Radius-1 statement is proved only for ₂F₁ (the p=q+1=2 case) via `ordinaryHypergeometricSeries_radius_eq_one`.

annotations.label: Divergence for p > q+1

anchor.snippet: If p > q + 1 then the ratio of coefficients grows without bound. This implies that, besides z = 0, the series diverges

annotations.note: No formalization of divergence in the p > q+1 regime.

annotations.label: Absolute convergence at z=1

anchor.snippet: It can be shown that the series converges absolutely at z = 1 if

annotations.note: No boundary convergence result for hypergeometric series at z=1 in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quigley convergence result

anchor.snippet: then the following convergence result holds Quigley et al. (2013)

annotations.note: Quigley's convergence criterion is not formalized.

annotations.label: Parameter symmetry and cancellation

anchor.snippet: the order of the parameters a j , or the order of the parameters b k can be changed without changing the value of the function

annotations.note: Only the a↔b swap for ₂F₁ is proved (`ordinaryHypergeometricSeries_symm`); general Sₚ × Sq symmetry isn't stated.

annotations.label: Reduction formula

anchor.snippet: This cancelling is a special case of a reduction formula that may be applied whenever a parameter on the top row differs from one on the bottom row by a non-negative integer

annotations.note: No reduction formula for pFq with cancelling parameters in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler's integral transform

anchor.snippet: The following basic identity is very useful as it relates the higher-order hypergeometric functions in terms of integrals over the lower order ones

annotations.note: Euler's integral representation for hypergeometric functions is not in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Derivative formulas for pFq (or even ₂F₁) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Contiguous function

anchor.snippet: A function obtained by adding ±1 to exactly one of the parameters

annotations.note: Contiguous-function terminology is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dixon's identity

anchor.snippet: Dixon's identity , [ 8 ] first proved by Dixon (1902) , gives the sum of a well-poised

annotations.note: Dixon's identity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dougall's formula

anchor.snippet: Dougall's formula ( Dougall 1907 ) gives the sum of a very well-poised series that is terminating and 2-balanced

annotations.note: Dougall's very-well-poised summation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss's hypergeometric functions (2F1)

anchor.snippet: These are sometimes called Gauss's hypergeometric functions , classical standard hypergeometric or often simply hypergeometric functions

annotations.note: `ordinaryHypergeometric` (notation `₂F₁`) is Gauss's classical hypergeometric function.

annotations.label: Hypergeometric differential equation

anchor.snippet: It is known as the hypergeometric differential equation

annotations.note: The Gauss ODE for ₂F₁ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Jacobi polynomials from 2F1

anchor.snippet: Up to constant factors and scaling, these are the Jacobi polynomials

annotations.note: Jacobi polynomials are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Integrals of elementary functions via 2F1

anchor.snippet: A wide range of integrals of elementary functions can be expressed using the hypergeometric function

annotations.note: No ₂F₁ representations of elementary integrals are formalized.

annotations.label: Roots of x^n - x + t via hypergeometric functions

anchor.snippet: the roots of the polynomial x n − x +t can be expressed as a sum of at most

annotations.note: No formalization of trinomial-root formulas via hypergeometric series.

annotations.label: Basic hypergeometric series (q-analog)

anchor.snippet: A generalization, the q-series analogues, called the basic hypergeometric series , were given by Eduard Heine in the late nineteenth century

annotations.note: Basic (q-)hypergeometric series are not in Mathlib (listed as TODO in `Pochhammer.lean`).

annotations.label: Elliptic hypergeometric series

anchor.snippet: Another generalization, the elliptic hypergeometric series , are those series where the ratio of terms is an elliptic function

annotations.note: Elliptic hypergeometric series are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bilateral hypergeometric series (generalization)

anchor.snippet: Bilateral hypergeometric series are a generalization of hypergeometric functions where one sums over all integers, not just the positive ones

annotations.note: No bilateral hypergeometric series in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fox–Wright functions

anchor.snippet: Fox–Wright functions are a generalization of generalized hypergeometric functions where the Pochhammer symbols in the series expression are generalised to gamma functions of linear expressions in the index n

annotations.note: Fox–Wright functions are not in Mathlib.
